title: Boundary anomalous dimensions from BCFT: $φ^{3}$ theories with a boundary and higher-derivative generalizations
abstract: We consider the bulk $φ^3$ deformation of the free boundary conformal field theory in the $ε$ expansion. We determine the leading corrections to the scaling dimensions of boundary fundamental operators and some boundary operator expansion coefficients. Our procedure combines the conformal multiplet recombination with the boundary crossing symmetry. The results cover both the single field case and the multi-field case with $S_{N+1}$ global symmetry, which are associated with the Yang-Lee model and the $(N+1)$-state Potts model respectively. These semi-infinite models describe branched polymers, percolation, and spanning forest at a surface. We generalize these results to some higher derivative theories. In addition, we study the $φ^{2n+1}$ theories with $n>1$, but only obtain some boundary operator expansion coefficients.
summary: This theoretical paper calculates the boundary anomalous dimensions and correlation function coefficients for $\phi^3$ theories in the presence of a boundary, extending the analysis to higher-derivative generalizations. It employs advanced CFT techniques, notably combining conformal multiplet recombination with boundary crossing symmetry, to determine critical exponents for models relevant to statistical physics, such as the Potts model.
main_problem: The paper determines the boundary anomalous dimensions and operator expansion coefficients for $\phi^3$ theories with a boundary, and generalizes these results to higher-derivative theories.
main_result: Specific formulas are derived for the anomalous dimensions ($\hat{\gamma}_\Phi$) and one-point function coefficients for both the single-field (Yang-Lee) and multi-field (Potts) cases, including generalizations to higher-derivative interactions.
method: The analysis combines the conformal multiplet recombination method with the boundary crossing symmetry applied to the bulk two-point correlator, often utilizing an $\epsilon$-expansion.
model_or_system: The primary models are $\phi^3$ deformations of free boundary conformal field theories (BCFT). Specific physical realizations include the Yang-Lee model (related to branched polymers) and the $(N+1)$-state Potts model (related to percolation).
key_observables: Boundary anomalous dimensions ($\hat{\gamma}_\Phi$), boundary operator expansion coefficients ($\mu_\Phi$), and bulk one-point function coefficients ($a_O$).
important_parameters: The analysis is performed in the $\epsilon$ expansion, and results depend on the coupling constants and boundary condition parameters ($\sigma_0, \sigma_1$).
main_assumptions: The calculations rely on the $\epsilon 	o 0$ limit and the assumption that certain contributions (like the bulk identity contribution) do not receive corrections at low orders in the expansion parameter $\xi$.
figures_summary: The notes reference tables summarizing main results for various $\phi^3$ CFTs and provide complex mathematical equations detailing the derived parameters and correlation functions.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the problem by first establishing the general framework using multiplet recombination and boundary crossing symmetry. It then applies this framework to specific models, such as the Yang-Lee and Potts models, before generalizing the results to higher-derivative theories ($\phi \Box^k \phi$) and $\phi^{2n+1}$ theories.
why_interesting: The rigorous application of CFT techniques like boundary crossing symmetry to determine critical exponents in interacting quantum field theories provides deep insights into universality classes relevant to critical phenomena in condensed matter systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
